rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Node kubernaut-demo-worker became NotReady due to kubelet failure, likely caused by a recurring cgroup misconfiguration (missing cpuset.cpus path at /sys/fs/cgroup/kubelet.slice/kubelet-kubepods.slice/cpuset.cpus) that ultimately caused the kubelet process to stop heartbeating. After the 60-second node-monitor-grace-period, all node conditions transitioned to Unknown and NoSchedule/NoExecute taints were applied, evicting all 13 pods from the node. The web-service Deployment in demo-node namespace is degraded at 2/3 replicas.
contributingFactors: Kubelet stopped posting heartbeats at 11:32:27Z; all node conditions transitioned to Unknown 60 seconds later (node-monitor-grace-period expiry)

Recurring FailedNodeAllocatableEnforcement warning (656 occurrences over ~11 hours): 'openat2 /sys/fs/cgroup/kubelet.slice/kubelet-kubepods.slice/cpuset.cpus: no such file or directory' — cgroup hierarchy incomplete/missing
Node runs on kind://podman (containerized Kubernetes), prone to cgroup v2 compatibility issues with cpuset subsystem
NoSchedule and NoExecute taints automatically applied at 11:33:27Z, evicting all workloads on the node
All 13 pods on the node simultaneously lost Ready=True at 11:33:27–28Z, confirming a single total-kubelet-failure event
web-service Deployment in demo-node namespace degraded: 2/3 replicas ready (MinimumReplicasUnavailable), one pod stuck on the unreachable node

After the 60-second node-monitor-grace-period, all node conditions transitioned to Unknown and NoSchedule/NoExecute taints were applied, evicting all 13 pods from the node. The web-service Deployment in demo-node namespace is degraded at 2/3 replicas.
